calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-21-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Nov-19-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Wendy Pun, #12891.

Jennifer E. Choi, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco, a Municipal Corporation, and the People of the State of California, by and through Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco.
David Herring, Building Inspector for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Kip Macy, an individual, Nicole Macy, an individual or Dana Street LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability corporation.
Defaults were entered against the defendants on September 18, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed October 16, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs City and County of San Francisco, a Municipal Corporation, and the People of the State of California, by and through Dennis J. Herrera, City Attorney for the City and County of San Francisco, and against the Defaulted Defendants Kip Macy, an individual, Nicole Macy, an individual or Dana Street LLC, a Nevada Limited Liability corporation, as follows:

Civil Penalties - $916,000.00, payable by December 19, 2009.

Judgment and Injunction were submitted to and signed by the Court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Continued To Dec-31-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recnder Terminating Sanction Of Default Judgemnt Against All Defts;
Off Calendar Sep-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jennifer E. Choi, Deputy City Attorney for for Plaintiff and moving party: City and County of San Francisco

No other appearance, Plaintiff's Motion to render terminating sanction of default judgment against all defendants granted in part:
1. The Court orders that defendants' answer be stricken;
2. The Clerk shall enter the default of all defendants as a terminating sanction for discovery abuse;
3. As to plaintiff's request for entry of judgment for equitable relief and monetary damages, plaintiff may contact the clerk of Department 218 to schedule a Prove-up hearing. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Continued To Oct-16-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To 1) Compel Discovery Responses, 2) Have Request For Admissions Deemed Admitted And 3) Impose Monetary Sanctions Against All Defts
Off Calendar Jul-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jennifer E. Choi, Deputy City Attorney, for Plaintiff and moving party: City and County of San Francisco
No other appearances, tentative ruling is adopted as modified. Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's Motion to compel discovery responses; have request for admissions deemed admitted; and to impose monetary sanctions against all defendants granted as follows:
a. Within seven days of the date of service of this order, each defendant--Kip Macy, Nicole Macy, and Dana Street LLC-- individually shall serve full, complete and verified responses to plaintiff's Form Interrogatories (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), and Request for Production of Documents (Set One), without objection;
b. As to plaintiff's request for admission, set one, to defendants Kip Macy, Nicole Macy, and Dana Street LLC, all matter set forth in each request for admission is deemed admitted;
c. Based on defendants' non-compliance with the Court's previous discovery order, and failure to respond to discovery requests, sanctions shall issue in the sum of $2,140.00, to run jointly and severally against defendants herein. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2009 Continued To Jul-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jennifer E. Choi, Deputy City Attorney, for Plaintiff and moving party: City and County of San Francisco

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's Motion to compel discovery responses from all defendants granted, as follows:
a. As to each responding defendant, all objections are waived, except for attorney-client and attorney work product privilege;
b. Within seven days of the date of service of this order, each defendant--Kip Macy, Nicole Macy, and Dana Street LLC-- individually shall serve full, complete and verified responses to plaintiff's Form Interrogatories (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), Requests for Admission (Set One), and Request for Production of Documents (Set One. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.
